data.body:
G3 (child coverage) — parent 5fd2 × child 7284 (standing eval suite): ONE partial-coverage finding.

4-bucket audit of this child's contribution to the parent's eval-suite AC ("STANDING per-criterion eval suite ... recall (bad→finding), false-accept (bad→pass), and false-fire (good→finding) cases per criterion"):
- all three corpuses (DSO/snap/rebar) → FULLY covered (corpus tags present)
- recall (bad→finding) cases → FULLY covered
- false-fire (good→finding) cases → FULLY covered (observed-FP taxonomy seeding)
- human-adjudicated gold subset + eval-gating → FULLY covered
- false-accept (bad→pass) case type → **PARTIALLY covered / case-type element UNCOVERED**

FINDING — the parent's eval-suite AC names THREE case types per criterion (recall / false-accept / false-fire); the child instantiates only TWO. Every `expect: pass` case in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml (FP1/FP4/FP5/FP6/T12-suppress/FP7) is a GOOD plan (false-fire); there is NO labeled bad-plan→pass (false-accept / rubber-stamp) case in any of the three specs. The inverse-of-recall signal exists only as the verifier's aggregate `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects` scorer (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml), not as a labeled per-criterion false-accept finder case the parent AC requires ("...cases per criterion"). Header comments + README advertise "false-accept (bad→pass)" but the dataset never realizes it, and the gate test buckets pass/false-accept together.

Sibling cross-check: 7284 is the SOLE owner of eval cases (681b owns the ISF criterion build, not its eval cases; acc1's Pass-2 discrimination cases were wired INTO 7284). No sibling backstops this — the absence holds across the whole roster.

Corroboration: this is the same gap the child's own two PASS-1 T8 reviews proved (named-but-never-instantiated third case-type / under-discriminating gate).

Suggested fix: either (a) add a labeled bad-plan→pass (false-accept) case per criterion so the parent's third case type is instantiated and testable, or (b) bind "false-accept" explicitly to the verifier sycophancy axis in the AC/README/header and stop advertising it as a finder case type — so the eval-suite criterion's coverage of the parent AC is complete and consistent.
